name: Empty States
description: What a section or page shows when it has nothing to display yet: a centered stack of a 24px icon, a bold title, a caption, and an optional secondary-button call to action, optionally inside a bordered container.

shared.layout: Centered column flex stack, text-align center. The empty state is placed centered inside whatever section has no content (usually a Primary card, see patterns/cards.json).
shared.sits_in: Typically centered inside a Primary card (white surface, 2px 2px 24px rgba(22,52,98,.08) shadow, 16px radius) that stands in for the page's missing content.

icon.note: Phosphor icon. Match the icon to the situation. Use CircleDashed as the neutral fallback when no situation-specific icon fits.

subtitle.note: The caption. This is the only element that survives in the Small size.

cta.note: Only add a CTA when there is a clear next step.

spec.layout: inline-flex, align-items center, gap 6, white-space nowrap
spec.note: iconEnd (e.g. an ArrowRight, 16px) is optional and sits after the label.

container.note: Bordered container wrapping the centered stack. Used inside a Primary card to frame the empty state.

Medium.role: The full model: icon + title + subtitle + optional CTA.
whenToUse: Where there is room to guide the user
When the empty state sits in an area with M-sized elements, such as admin pages (use the Medium CTA)

Small.role: Subtitle only. Drops the icon, title, and CTA, leaving just the 12px caption.
whenToUse: Cramped or dense sections where only the empty message fits, such as a dropdown panel or a tight subsection
When the surrounding area uses small components, such as a config panel (pair with a Small CTA if a CTA is genuinely needed)

scenarios.label: First use / no data yet
scenarios.example: Trained Models: Stack icon, title "No trained models yet", subtitle "Each training run adds a version here.", CTA "Go to Classes" (Small, with an ArrowRight)

scenarios.label: No results
scenarios.example: Workflow node search: FlowArrow icon, title "No Results", subtitle "There are no nodes matching your search.", no CTA

guidelines: Use a centered stack to explain why a section has no content and what action fills it
The same model covers every no-content situation; match the icon and copy to the situation
Only add a CTA when there is a clear next step
Match the size to the components around the empty state
Medium (full model) where there is room to guide the user; use a Medium CTA in areas with M-sized elements such as admin pages
Small (subtitle only) for limited space such as a dropdown panel or a tight subsection; use a Small CTA when the surrounding area uses small components such as a config panel
Fall back to the CircleDashed icon when no situation-specific icon fits

themes.dark: not documented; the pattern page keeps example mockups light
